rules.description: An offer must clearly state intent to be bound, specific terms, and be communicated directly to the offeree.
An offer remains open for 72 hours from the time of communication unless explicitly revoked sooner or a specific expiry is stated within the offer itself.
Acceptance is effective only upon explicit written confirmation received by the offeror. Silence or performance alone does not constitute acceptance.
Any modification to an offer, however minor, constitutes a counter-offer and terminates the original offer.
Consideration must be reciprocal and tangible. Sentimental value, promises of future good behavior, or past actions do not qualify.
Consideration must have a stated minimum value of $100.
A breach occurs if a party fails to perform any material term of the contract as agreed, or if anticipatory repudiation is explicitly stated in writing.
Failure to perform due to unforeseen 'Act of God' events (e.g., natural disaster, war declared by government) is excused if a 'force majeure' clause is absent.

Damages for breach are limited to direct monetary losses actually incurred and specifically proven, capped at the lesser of 150% of the contract value or $10,000. Consequential damages are never awarded.
Specific performance is only available for contracts involving unique intellectual property or real estate, and only if monetary damages are demonstrably inadequate.

events.content: We accept your offer for 100 widgets at $150 each, but delivery will be by 2023-02-20.

events.description: Started manufacturing widgets based on the offer.

events.content: We are no longer interested in the widgets.

dispute_claim.reason: DDI breached contract by canceling after AWC started manufacturing.
